chapters.title: Not by recycling alone
chapters.text:
People with fossil fuel interests have been actively working for decades to discredit and disregard warnings about the warming of our planet. They use all resources at their disposal to prevent policies aimed at curbing pollution and planetary warming from sailing through.

Learning from the gun lobby’s motto — guns don’t kill people, people kill people — and the tobacco industry’s strategies for absolving themselves of responsibility, the fossil fuel industry has repeatedly avoided and prevented action toward preserving the planet. The Koch brothers, the Mercers, the Scaifes, and companies like ExxonMobil have spent billions of dollars to misinform the public about climate change. They discredit the science that links fossil fuel burning to climate change. Their strategy has worked so far.

Climate change is a principal symptom of the possible calamity, not its reason.

To tackle this problem, we need to do three things:

• Acknowledge that we are in a war.
• Understand how the enemy’s mind works.
• Develop a battle plan to overcome these hurdles and save our planet.

In this summary, we will unveil the obstructive maneuvers and postponement schemes that powerful stakeholders implement to thwart climate progress. You will also discover the strategies of the enemy and the steps we must apply to squelch these lies. Follow through and join the fight for the life of our planet.

chapters.title: Climate misinformation that can kill us all
chapters.text:
Oil-funded governments, fossil fuel companies, and right-wing plutocrats who depend on fossil fuels cannot continue to deny the science; what was forecasted is already happening. These behemoths have developed a new tactic — shifting from denial to deception, distraction, and delay.

While personal action is necessary, it cannot avert the impending doom. The deflection campaign has created a rift in the climate advocacy community between those who favor personal action and those pushing for policy action.

Another weapon these forces of inaction use is to oppose regulations of carbon emission and attack renewable energy solutions. They propose false remedies like carbon capture, geoengineering, and future innovations that will save us.

A third strategy is PSYOP. They tell people climate change impacts will not be as harmful as the science states. This narrative dampens any sense of urgency and agency. This strategy is bolstered by those who claim that nothing can be done to correct the course.

With over two decades of experience fighting this war, Mann proposes a plan to save our planet from a climate crisis:

1. Disregard the misguided belief that the planet is doomed already.
2. Follow the idealism of the youngest generation who are trying to save their planet.
3. Educate the honest, confused folks who are victims of the climate change misinformation campaign.
4. Support leaders willing to make systemic change instead of shifting the responsibility to individuals.

The Earth requires mindful actions, not fears, preconceptions, and anxiety.

Understand that this climate war is yours to fight. Convert the fear and anxiety into motivation and action. We have a home where the conditions are perfect for our survival. We have not found any other planet with the same conditions. Let us strive to preserve it with everything we have. It is a crime against the Earth, not just humanity, for some people to continue to hurt our world for monetary gains. It is unethical, immoral, and unacceptable.

chapters.title: Science under siege
chapters.text:
The enemy sets out to discredit the messenger by attacking their personality. When science clashes with industrial or corporate interests, the scientist is dubbed “an enemy of the people.” Since the industry has more money than scientists, they get more attention.

In 1969, a Brown and Williamson executive candidly declared, “Doubt is our product.” The tobacco company hid the health threats of smoking from the public. Frederick Seitz was a renowned solid-state physicist who collected USD 500,000 from R.J. Reynolds, a tobacco giant. Seitz was tasked with leveraging his scientific reputation to dispute any established connections between tobacco and health issues.

When a scientist makes a finding that affects an industry, the giants in the industry resort to character assassination to discredit them. What hurts is that scientists in other fields are often employed to deny the results of their fellow professionals.

Three scientists are best described as free-market fundamentalists. They are Frederick Seitz, Robert Jastrow, and William Nierenberg. With lavish funding from the tobacco industry, they undermined the efforts to regulate the activities of corporations that were hurting the planet. They established the George C. Marshall Institute (GMI), which specialized in denying any scientific finding that negatively impacts their benefactors — the military-industrial complex. They denied acid rain and ozone depletion despite the indisputable facts that said otherwise. They also supported President Reagan’s Strategic Defense Initiative that held the threat of a nuclear winter. Designed as an anti-missile system, the SDI aimed to intercept nuclear missiles in space. At this time, the US was in an arms race against the Soviet Union.

One scientist who was a match for the GMI trio was Carl Sagan. He was a Cornell University professor specializing in Astronomy and Space Sciences. He was an affable scientist who educated the public with science in a way they could easily understand. His popular PBS series “Cosmos” enabled him to become the voice of science for the nation.

Politics, communication, and the Internet are the tools we can use to improve the world.

When the Cold War ended in the late 1980s, the GMI shifted attention to acid rain and ozone depletion in the early 1990s. When they could no longer hide the facts of acid rain and ozone depletion, they shifted attention to climate change — all in an attempt to keep milking their benefactors.

Did you know? Frederick Seitz is the grandfather of denialism enlisted by the tobacco industry in its war on science.

chapters.title: When truth is not profitable
chapters.text:
Before we got better instruments, the science community did agree on how humans affected the climate. James Hansen, the director of the NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies, testified to Congress in June 1988 that greenhouse gasses resulting from human activities were warming the planet. Unfortunately, he didn’t get sufficient backing from the science community at the time due to the discovery of the phenomenon of “chaos.”

Chaos states that we cannot predict the weather beyond a week.

The White House downplayed Hansen’s testimony by altering it. What was unclear at this time was the human impact on the climate. There was broad consensus that burning fossil fuels and releasing greenhouse gasses were warming the planet. Yet, the forces of inaction were focused on exploiting the loophole created by a lack of consensus on the human role.

The fossil fuel industry knew President George H.W. Bush was about to take policy action to address climate change. Therefore, Nierenberg presented the dismissive view of climate change prepared by the GMI to the White House to cause division among them and stall the action they were about to take.

When the United Nations set up the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) in 1988, the GMI could not fight against climate action alone. They got help from a consortium of fossil fuel interests called the Global Climate Coalition. ExxonMobil, Shell, British Petroleum, the American Petroleum Institute, and others came together in 1989 to challenge the basic science of climate change. Their arguments were:

• The science was uncertain.
• The models were unreliable.
• The data had too many errors.
• The role of natural variability is unknown, and we cannot establish any clear human role.

The Koch brothers — David and Charles Koch — funded the first known climate change denial conference in June 1991. At this conference, the GMI trio got two new recruits — Richard S. Lindzen from MIT and S. Fred Singer.

By 1995, there was compelling evidence for human-caused climate change. In the November 1995 report of the IPCC, where the current consensus would be summarized, there was a two-day debate on whether to use the word “appreciable” or “discernable” to describe the human impact on climate change. Saudi Arabia and Kuwait had sponsored an attack against the new consensus for their economic interests. By February 1996, there were threats to the career and life of Ben Santer, one of the scientists who co-authored the IPCC report.

chapters.title: Hockey stick heat in the climate combat
chapters.text:
In 1997, countries committed to substantial reductions in carbon emissions in what was dubbed the Kyoto Protocol. The forces of denial responded with the “Oregon Petition,” which they got 31,000 nominal scientists to sign. When the Scientific American contacted the scientists who signed, many of them indicated that they no longer supported it.

The “hockey stick” article was released on April 22, 1998, to describe the unrivaled nature of modern global warming. It featured in the Third Assessment Report of the IPCC as the new evidence for climate change. The hockey stick curve supports the conclusion that a recent temperature increase has not been experienced in the past 1,000 years.

For the last two decades, scores of independent research teams have verified the hockey stick findings. These studies confirm that the recent warming has been unprecedented in the last two millennia and possibly the past 20,000 years.

Denying the truth or covering lies is the perfect recipe for possible catastrophe.

Still, ideologically and agenda-driven “alternative facts” have displaced reality and logic. The last-ditch effort to deny climate change by vested interests was Climategate. This incident refers to the manipulation of stolen emails to sway the Copenhagen Summit in December 2009.

When the reality of climate change could no longer be denied, the forces of inaction decided to evade the matter. During the Trump administration, the US joined forces with Russia and Saudi Arabia to dilute climate commitments, as detailed by The Guardian on December 9, 2018. They use the Murdoch media empire — owners of Fox News — and fake reports sponsored by the fossil fuel industry to misinform the public.

Nowadays, denialists tend to minimize the consequences of climate change rather than completely reject the evidence supporting it. However, climate change threatens the US economy by more than a trillion dollars annually.

From denialism, the forces of inaction — best described as inactivists — are shifting base to become deceivers and dissemblers. Dissemblers are those who downplay, deflect, divide, delay, and preach doom. Their goal is to use a multifaceted approach to deflect blame, create discord among the public, postpone genuine action by advocating for alternative measures that are largely ineffective, or encourage resignation to our destiny. These new inactivists are the foot soldiers of the new climate war. We must know them and learn to spot their strategies if we are going to preserve our planet.

chapters.title: Deflection over action
chapters.text:
A classic example of a deflection campaign is the slogan used by the National Rifle Association (NRA): “Guns don’t kill people, people kill people.” Instead of dealing with easy access to assault weapons, this slogan makes it appear as if mass shootings are due to some other issues, such as mental illness. This slogan has stalled gun reforms.

The Crying Indian ad is another example of a deflection campaign. The advert’s message is that people start pollution and people can stop it. It tells us that individuals can solve the problem of environmental degradation if we get our act together and put our minds to it.

Systemic changes must complement individual actions to effectively tackle climate change.

The government must hold carbon polluters accountable. But when the focus is on individual voluntary action, the drive to regulate carbon pollution reduces. It is essential to strike a careful balance between individual accountability and pressuring lawmakers to enact climate-friendly state measures.

Perpetrators of deflection campaigns are not interested in solving the problem. They aim to prevent any systemic solutions that may hurt financial interests. Fossil fuel interests and climate inactivists launch multi pronged attacks to block any move toward regulating carbon emissions.

Climate inactivists also sow a seed of discord among climate advocates by generating conflict among them over the right course of action to curb climate change. These vested interests also fire up conservative politicians by focusing on the personal sacrifices that climate action may require.

They blame climate change on everything but the industry. These inactivists blame what we eat, how we move around, whether we should vacation, and shame our behavior for the environmental crisis. This deflective tactic has been so effective that when you query the typical person about halting global warming, they suggest going vegan, switching off the lights, and perhaps petitioning your representatives.

chapters.title: Not by recycling alone
chapters.text:
The wedge strategy capitalizes on existing fractures within any large, heterogeneous collective by emphasizing the points of disagreement. The goal is to prevent them from speaking with one voice.

Social media is a battlefield for climate change.

These dividers use professional trolls on social media to amplify a meme, and an army of bots to increase the amplification until genuine individuals buy into their agenda. This strategy was how Russiagate happened. Russia was wary of what a Clinton presidency might do about fossil fuels. Consequently, they tampered with the 2016 election by dividing the Democrats using social media. They made the younger generation believe that Clinton and Trump shared the same opinion on climate change, and that there was no difference between them. They amplified this notion to depress the number of young democrats who voted.

Given social media’s predisposition to conflict, these dividers manipulate blame-casting, lifestyle-shaming, virtue-displaying, and airing of grievances to escalate minor disagreements into significant schisms.

A study found that focusing on desirable behaviors instead of negative norms is better at driving change. Aggressive guilt-based engagement results in the boomerang effect. What these dividers seek is inaction, so mission accomplished.

To squelch the gimmicks of dividers, we need to stop feeding the trolls on social media. Avoid divisive rants about carbon sins, accusations of hypocrisy about climate change, and censuring of meat consumption. Be constructive in your engagement with others on climate matters to prevent the boomerang effect. While trying to correct dangerous lies about climate change, you must be vigilant to avoid traps set by trolls and bots.

Understand that recycling your bottles or choosing sailing over flying is not sufficient action against climate change. Advocate for broader reforms by backing groups that prioritize climate issues and politicians who endorse measures to tax pollution.

chapters.title: Climate crossroads
chapters.text:
On the supply side, we must halt the construction of pipelines, forbid fracking, cease the practice of mountain-top removal for coal extraction, divest from fossil fuel enterprises, and obstruct the establishment of new fossil fuel facilities. On the demand side, we must opt for climate-friendly energy, transportation, and agricultural practices that are less expensive but more efficient than fossil fuel options.

Just as personal action is no substitute for systemic change, supply-side efforts are no substitute for demand-side approaches. Both are necessary. ~ Michael E. Mann

Carbon pricing is an effective way to ensure the balance of scale in reducing the carbon footprint. Initiatives for carbon pricing entail imposing a carbon tax on polluters and rewarding carbon reduction efforts with credits.

Awareness and knowledge are the first steps to tackling climate change.

A 2009 carbon-pricing bill in the US was blocked by fossil fuel interests and right-wing anti-regulation plutocrats. In Australia, similar legislation suffered setbacks because of climate change denial leadership.

Following ratifying a cap-and-trade provision to the Clean Air Act by former Republican president George H.W. Bush in 1990, sulfur emissions from coal-powered plants fell by 36% from 1990 to 2004, even with a 25% hike in electricity generation. The GOP overturned t his environmental success story.

Did you know? Allegations persist that Russia played a role in the 2018 “Yellow Vest” disturbances in France, which disrupted government attempts to implement a carbon tax.

chapters.title: Tesla or the modern climate warrior
chapters.text:
According to the International Monetary Fund, the fossil fuel industry receives up to $5 trillion in explicit and implicit subsidies globally. This industry got these subsidies by spending a whopping $354 million on lobbying and campaign contributions. At the same time, they deploy industry proxy groups such as the American Legislative Exchange Council (ALEC) and the Heartland Institute to undermine initiatives that would benefit their rivals in renewable energy at every level.

One major assault on renewable energy is the attack on electric vehicles (EVs). EVs play a vital role in decarbonizing the transportation sector. In 2015, the Koch siblings conspired with companies specializing in oil refining and distribution to initiate a multi-million-dollar campaign against EVs.

Tesla may be the greatest threat of all to the fossil fuel industry. ~ Michael E. Mann

These sleek automobiles outsold high-performance conventional vehicles in North Carolina so much that Tesla sales had to be banned in Charlotte. Republican Governor Chris Christie tried to ban it in New Jersey, and some other red states towed the same line.

These inactivists have whipped up sentiments against renewable energy. Some of them include the claim that renewable energy will take away people’s jobs, is not affordable for the poor, and compromises our health and environment. When these gimmicks fail, their last-ditch claim is that there are insufficient storage facilities for energy from renewable sources.

Using present-day renewable energy and technologies, we could fulfill up to 80% of the world's energy needs by 2030 and achieve full sufficiency by 2050. It includes electrification of energy systems, decarbonizing electricity grids with a diverse mix of power sources, installation of onshore and offshore wind turbines, harnessing wave and geothermal power, and (to top it off), utilizing hydroelectric and tidal energies.

What we need is the political will and economic incentives to get it done — if vested interests stop sabotaging.

By decentralizing power generation, more people will have access to energy.

Switching to renewable energy is projected to generate millions of jobs, ensure stable energy prices by eliminating fuel costs, and minimize power outages by integrating diverse energy sources.

Germany's solar power sector outperforms that of the US despite having a solar potential akin to Alaska’s. The reason is that it is not afflicted by the Murdoch media influence, the Koch siblings, and fossil fuel lobbyists.

You need to equip yourself with factual data to dispel the falsehoods surrounding the purported environmental risks of wind turbines and solar panels.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
The path we must take to preserve global climate involves energy efficiency, electrification, and grid decarbonization using renewable energy sources that complement one another. These solutions do not profit fossil fuel interests, so they’ve used their wealth to frustrate any efforts in the right direction. These vested interests and their advocates began with the denial of climate change. When denial failed, they switched to deflecting attention from real climate solutions by promoting ostensible alternatives.

When deflection fails, they enlist the help of some climate activists to preach doom and gloom, asking us to accept fate because we cannot do anything to stop the impending disaster.

We must not fall into this trap. Rather, we should broadcast the risks and potential of the climate predicament. We must foster an inspiring hope based on credible and defensible evidence for moderate optimism that we can still prevent the worst outcomes. The misconception that it is too late for action is a direct route to ensuring disastrous climate outcomes. Framing climate change as a climate emergency imparts both a sense of urgency and the potential for action. A crisis is a time when a vital decision must be made.

We must eschew self-doubt and imbibe the belief that we have what it takes to meet this challenge — to win this climate war despite the mammoth-sized monsters and their weaponry.

Try this



• Take a stand by supporting efforts that demand systemic change to tackle the climate crisis.
• Use your social media handles to correct misinformation on the viability of renewable energy sources.
• Demonstrate agency by making choices in your personal life that inspire others to do something about the climate crisis. We can win if we try.
